messages.text: Looks like this is what the settings were at when we were running fine on Saturday
Yes I know all the settings thank you. We are consistently low which has not happened for months. It's just a matter of dialing in with  equipment that is functioning correctly now.
Maintenance continues to make adjustments to the mixing water.
Robert Villarreal let us know if there is something else that we should change.
Robert had Nathan change to 115 instead of 118 for pretreat on Friday late afternoon. It has been at 115 since 6:20. I am following the process for low slope. It's getting better.
Robert Villarreal seems like it wants more normal numbers.
Maintenance continues to make adjustments to the mixing water.
First good temper in the yellow -0.3.
Latest test is -0.1 ... Ended up 1.3 over Saturday's settings on both mixing water and crystalization. 82.3 on mixing and 80.5 on crystalization. All other settings unchanged.

Tempering chart test check update 


We begin depositing chocolate after the initial tempering stage, once the reading meets the center line requirements.
Snake eyes on the last one.
Product is out

Organoleptic test check update 


The product's appearance looks good; its odor and texture are acceptable, falling within the permissible ranges and limits required by the product specifications.

Check weight test check update 


The weight looks good; we are within the parameters required by the product specifications.
Despite infeed being very clean, we are still having chocolate cut and open FW
We stopped sending chocolate to the hopper we have a high Temper
Operator make an adjustment to the Mixing Water.
Belt C6 is running good
The metal detector belt is running good
Maintenance adjusted the mixing water at 9:37 AM.

Grey Boscanl Rich Brierley

What speed are we running?
While checking the flow-wrap packaging, I found 60 open units out of a box of 1,050 an average open-unit rate of 5.7%.
If we can’t get to 2% or less I want to slow down to 15 MPm
0.2° Change made on the first +0.8 didn't bring it down I made a 0.5° change 5 min ago. Not depositing at the time.
16mpm
Found some slop in the chain. Preston tightened it up. Frederick Peairs is going to help us collect some data to have a little more to go off as for improvements/changes.
I think the centerline form is updated to have a slightly different crystallization temperature Rich/Grey

We have reduced to 15mpm and have shut Bosch down to help with lunch rotation. We have 4 EE's checking the product this includes operator and Lead, we have only added 1 extra EE for inspection of the product.
This is what I see the temperature should be

Chad Stone Rich Brierley Rich Brierley Shawn Jewkes The cooler is empty and the temper is still bad. We have sent the crew to lunch and if temper becomes within range we will fill the cooler while EE's are on lunch
We started with that this morning and it wasn't working. Started with what was on the CL. Can you read the rest of the chat and catch up?
I'm all caught up on the chat - I don't see anywhere mentioned adjustments to crystallization
Ok I'll do it
Those settings gave us 5 good tests, -0.3, -0.1, 0.0, 0.1, 0.1 and then bam a +0.8
Maintenance adjusted the mixing water at 10:11 AM.

I know this is challenging and frustrating at the same time. 

I appreciate everyone continuing to document and working at it.
Filling hopper
We are depositing chocolate

Preston Elmer 
Shot back up to 0.9 on the most recent temper
Product is out
Stopped sending chocolate to the hopper, high Temper
ay Dios mio

We have a small inconsistency in the shades of the boxes, one is darker from the other, so the next step is to separate the light from the joints, they cannot be mixed, the leader was mentioned and we are already separating the light ones in a different pallet and the dark ones in another pallet.
We started sending chocolate to the hopper, we have two acceptable Temper

We performed some quantitative tests on the cavanna. 
At cut position 214, in 3:30, we found 16 bad seals. 
At 212, we found 10 in 3:36
At 210, there were 0 for 3:01, then we paused for product gap. Starting back from the pause we saw a bunch together, then 1 in the next 30 seconds
At 208, ran 1:15 with zero bads before running out of product
We started sending everything  we can to bosch while they work on belt issue
5 found total in 6 minutes at steady state

Chad Stone Rich Brierley Imperial  Nathan Gevenois Grey Boscan   Crew remember if Cavanna goes down due to issues you can send product to Bosch and keep the line running. Its better to run something then shutting the line down and running nothing
Upon re-checking the flow-wrap packaging, I found 70 open units out of a box of 1,050—an average open-unit rate of 6.6%.
Belts C6 and Metal Detector are running correctly

Check weight test check update 


The weight looks good; we are within the parameters required by the product specifications.

Organoleptic test check update 


The product's appearance looks good; its odor and texture are acceptable, falling within the permissible ranges and limits required by the product specifications.
Tempering chart test check update temperature is very stable and has remained within the acceptable range required by the Centerline.
We have issues with Belt C9 is melting the chocolate, maintenance is in the line

Maintenance was called over because there was lots of melted chocolate on the c9 belt. I found this valve to be closed so we haven’t been circulating cold water through the chill plates under the belts I opened the valve and the plates all started to cool down
This valve is not part of the Cavanna startup checklist. An action will be added to update the checklist and verify that the valve is open before startup. Frederick Peairs

I am checking the flow-wrap results following the latest adjustments made to this box—which weighs 27.8 and contains exactly 1,056 units—and found only 3 open packages, representing a rate of 0.28%; we are well below the 1% threshold for open packages per box. 


 


Flow wrap ckeck update 


The flow-wrap packaging is in good condition; it looks good and is very well sealed.
Metal detector belt, chain and belt of imperial, Demolding and c6 belt are running correctly
Glycol is good
Nathan Gevenois

The operator of MM8 was checking why the product wasn't dropping correctly into the pouches when they noticed one of the bag-opening fingers had fallen off. The operator stopped the Matrix, and upon inspection, we realized the part had snapped. The operator, QA and I we examined it, and the piece appears to be complete; maintenance is on the way to inspect it and remove the remaining section. Matrix 8 only runs intermittently because it rarely receives enough product. 
Dakota Adair Angelica Perez

Similarly, the necessary X-ray checks are being conducted every hour, and no bags containing metal have been rejected so far! Each rejected bag is being re-scanned twice as required, but so far nothing has been found.
Thank you for the details.
Together with the supervisor, we have verified that no parts are missing that could compromise the product
Could you please send one of the broken pieces so we can see if it's recognized by the x-ray machine? Delia Zambrano Elizabeth Hernandez
For the moment, the MM'8 ​​will only have three fingers for opening the pouches; maintenance will check if they have the parts needed to replace it.
She put the item in a bag and it was successfully returned
Thank you

The caramel tank reserve is below 50%; we're going to have to pause to bring the percentage back up.

We performed another verification to determine how long it takes for the buffer tank to empty, and it dropped by 20% in 30 minutes. The lead will stop filling the tanks at 8:00 a.m., and we will continue monitoring. At this time, we estimate that second shift will be able to run for approximately 1 to 1.5 hours. We will verify the tank levels again at midshift.

The drop weight started at 5.8; the pre-piece showed results of 9.4 to 9.9, while the final piece was at 22.0. The operator was informed and made adjustments to the drop and the chocolate to achieve the desired weights.
Caramel drop was adjusted to the minimun allowed weight (5.5), sprinkler roller was adjusted from 5% to 3%, and shaker was increased from 70% to 85%. I will closely monitor the Russell finex.
